paragraphs.text: Munich, 24th of October 2025

section_path: Usercentrics Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence – Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: Usercentrics, provider of both Usercentrics CMP and Cookiebot CMP, welcomes the European Commission's initiative to strengthen consumer protection and trust in the digital environment through the forthcoming Digital Fairness Act.

section_path: Usercentrics Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence – Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: As a leading European provider of Consent Management Platforms (CMP), we strongly support the Commission's efforts to address manipulative design patterns, deceptive interface practices, and other forms of digital unfairness that undermine user autonomy.

section_path: Usercentrics Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence – Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: Digital fairness and privacy are deeply connected: both rely on transparency, informed choice, and user control. We therefore view this initiative as a vital opportunity to align consumer protection law and data-protection principles under a shared vision of fairness-by-design.

section_path: Usercentrics Response to the European Commission Call for Evidence – Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act should build on the EU's proven privacy-by-design approach to embed fairness directly into digital products and services. Designing for fairness means ensuring that consent, personalization, and commercial interactions are understandable, contextual, and free of manipulation.

section_path: Privacy by Design as the Foundation of Digital Fairness

paragraphs.text: CMPs already operationalize these principles in millions of daily interactions—making data practices transparent, ensuring that consent is freely given, and providing auditable records of user choice.

section_path: Privacy by Design as the Foundation of Digital Fairness

evidence_quotes.quote: Rather than prescribing visual templates or fixed layouts,

paragraphs.text: Rather than prescribing visual templates or fixed layouts, we encourage the Commission to adopt an outcome-based framework: regulate what fairness must achieve, not how it must look.

section_path: Privacy by Design as the Foundation of Digital Fairness

paragraphs.text: Such an approach safeguards both creativity and legal certainty, allowing innovative UX solutions to flourish within a clear, enforceable set of principles.

section_path: Privacy by Design as the Foundation of Digital Fairness

paragraphs.text: Usercentrics is actively contributing to evidence-based policymaking through a government-funded research project focused on transparency and user understanding. The project explores how to make the concept of personalized advertising easier to explain to end

section_path: Privacy by Design as the Foundation of Digital Fairness

paragraphs.text: users while ensuring full legal compliance and maintaining high privacy standards. By combining empirical insights with our operational experience, we aim to help define practical, fair, and privacy-preserving standards for digital design.

section_path: Privacy by Design as the Foundation of Digital Fairness

paragraphs.text: Usercentrics particularly welcomes the Commission's recognition that the protection of minors must be a transversal priority of the Digital Fairness Act.

section_path: Protection of Minors and Age-Appropriate Design

paragraphs.text: Children and teenagers are early adopters of digital products but also among the most vulnerable to manipulative and addictive design. In practice, effective safeguards must reflect how digital services are actually used: many can be accessed without a login, and devices such as tablets or smart TVs, are often shared within families. In such cases, neither account-based nor device-level age verification can reliably identify the actual user.

section_path: Protection of Minors and Age-Appropriate Design

paragraphs.text: We therefore support measures that promote age-appropriate transparency, contextual consent flows, and parental oversight mechanisms. To be effective, these measures should remain low-friction and seamlessly integrated into the user experience, ensuring strong protection without unnecessary barriers or complexity that might otherwise encourage users to circumvent safeguards or shift toward less compliant services.

section_path: Protection of Minors and Age-Appropriate Design

evidence_quotes.quote: privacy-preserving approaches to age verification and age-appropriate design

paragraphs.text: Usercentrics supports the development of privacy-preserving approaches to age verification and age-appropriate design that protect minors without increasing data exposure or creating new identifiers. We encourage the Commission to promote innovation in this area, including solutions that build on existing European trust frameworks such as the EU Digital Identity and Digital Wallets, to ensure that protection of minors can be achieved in a way that is both effective and privacy-respecting.

section_path: Protection of Minors and Age-Appropriate Design

paragraphs.text: True fairness requires that consumers understand and can act on their choices across services, devices, and borders.
To achieve this, Europe needs interoperable trust infrastructure, not identical interfaces.

evidence_quotes.quote: consent preferences, age-status signals, and fairness indicators can move securely between controllers while preserving autonomy and accountability.

paragraphs.text: Interoperability ensures that transparency travels with the user: consent preferences, age-status signals, and fairness indicators can move securely between controllers while preserving autonomy and accountability.

evidence_quotes.quote: common standards for consent and fairness signaling

paragraphs.text: Usercentrics therefore supports the creation of common standards for consent and fairness signaling—a shared "language" that enables different systems to communicate transparently without prescribing their internal logic or UI design.
Such an approach reduces fragmentation, strengthens enforcement, and fosters innovation among European privacy-tech providers.

We agree that education and awareness are essential complements to regulation. Citizens must understand their digital rights, but technology should also embody those rights through fair defaults and intuitive interaction models.
Privacy-by-design and education are therefore mutually reinforcing: one embeds protection into systems, the other embeds it into society.

evidence_quotes.quote: We share the view that over-standardization could lead to user fatigue and habituation.

paragraphs.text: Usercentrics recognizes that Europe's strength lies in its diversity of ideas and interfaces. We share the view that over-standardization could lead to user fatigue and habituation.
However, consistency in principles, not uniformity in design, is what ensures fairness at scale.
The Commission should define clear criteria for manipulative or deceptive practices, while allowing businesses to implement compliant, user-friendly designs that fit their brand and audience.

A Digital Fairness Act grounded in privacy by design can strengthen Europe's leadership in ethical digital transformation. It can create a level playing field for responsible businesses while protecting vulnerable users and restoring trust in digital interactions.

section_path: Toward a Fair, Transparent and Inclusive Digital Market

paragraphs.text: Fairness and innovation are not opposing goals; they are interdependent.

section_path: Toward a Fair, Transparent and Inclusive Digital Market

paragraphs.text: By combining privacy-enhancing technologies, interoperable standards, and clear UX principles, Europe can ensure that every citizen's digital experience is transparent, respectful, and empowering.

section_path: Toward a Fair, Transparent and Inclusive Digital Market

section_path: Toward a Fair, Transparent and Inclusive Digital Market

paragraphs.text: Digital fairness starts with privacy by design. Europe can be a world leader by making transparency, user control, and protection of minors integral to every digital experience. Usercentrics believes that fairness and innovation are not opposing goals but two sides of a trusted digital economy — one that empowers citizens, supports responsible businesses, and turns compliance into confidence.

Usercentrics remains open to further exchange and would welcome active engagement between the Commission's initiative team and Consent Management Platforms to provide concrete input from real-world implementation experience. Collaboration between regulators, CMPs, and industry stakeholders will be essential to ensure that digital fairness is achieved in practice. This means empowering users, supporting responsible businesses, and strengthening trust across the digital economy.
